questions.question: Charice creates a new kind of glue using a new chemical formula that she invented. She wants to make sure that none of her competitors use the formula for their products. To protect the formula, Charice should __________ it.
questions.explanation: Patent. Patents protect inventions and prevent others from making, using, or selling that invention. A new chemical formula for glue is a new invention, so Charice should obtain a patent to protect it. Trademarks protect the names of products and prevent other companies from using those names for their own products. Copyrights protect original works, such as music, paintings, literary works, and television. A prototype is an early test or sample of a product, not a form of protection for intellectual property. 11, 2015, from http://www.alllaw.com/articles/intellectual_property/article5.asp
sources: SOURCE:	 Hinson, C. (2015). Protecting your intellectual property: How and why. Retrieved November

questions.question: Josh is a small business owner who hires a lawyer to represent him in court. In this situation, Josh is the
questions.explanation: Principal. In this situation, Josh is the principal, while his lawyer is the agent. In an agency relationship, the agent has authority to act on behalf of the principal. Josh is not a judge or a representative. http://smallbusiness.chron.com/principalagent-relationship-32117.html
sources: SOURCE:	 Green, J. (2015). What is a principal-agent relationship? Retrieved November 30, 2015, from

questions.question: Which distribution method is commonly used for consumer shopping goods, such as high-end clothing:
questions.explanation: Selective. Selective distribution means selling a product through a limited number of wholesalers and retailers in a geographic area. Marketers use this method when they want to deal with the middlemen they feel will do the best job of promoting and selling their products. This method is often used with consumer shopping goods, such as high-end clothing. Intensive distribution means selling a product through every available wholesaler and retailer in a geographic area where consumers might look for it. Marketers use this method when they are attempting to reach the greatest number of consumers possible. Convenience products are often distributed this way. Exclusive distribution means selling a product through just one middleman in a geographic area. Marketers use this method when they need to maintain tight control over a product. This is why exclusive distribution is often used for specialty products that are technical in nature or require specialized services such as installation or repair. Direct distribution refers to eliminating middlemen and dealing with final consumers directly. This method is not often used for high-end clothing.
sources: SOURCE:	 CM LAP 2—Chart Your Channels (Channel Management)

questions.question: Which follow-up tool automatically returns a specified message to anyone who emails a particular address:
questions.explanation: Autoresponder. An autoresponder is a computer program that automatically returns a specified message to anyone who emails a particular address. Customer support responses typically come from a customer service representative. The FAQ section of a website is a static section on the website that includes answers to frequent questions. Order processing is the fulfilling of an order of goods or services placed by the customer. December 1, 2015, from http://www.web-source.net/followup.htm#.Vl2ur3arSM-
sources: SOURCE:	 Lowery, S. (n.d.). Part 10: Customer service-Following up with your customers. Retrieved

questions.question: For industrial goods, the equivalent of a wholesaler is a(n)
questions.explanation: Industrial distributor. For industrial goods, the equivalent of a wholesaler is an industrial distributor. This is an intermediary that buys large quantities of goods from a producer and breaks them down into smaller portions to sell to retailers—or, sometimes, directly to industrial users. An industrial distributor might be considered a business partner but is not called such. A producer makes or provides goods or services. A retailer is a business that buys consumer goods or services and sells them to ultimate consumers. 16
sources: SOURCE:	 CM LAP 1—Channel It (Channels of Distribution)

questions.question: Which of the following businesses is most likely to violate antitrust laws if it insists on an exclusive dealing agreement:

options.A: A firm with dominant market power

questions.explanation: A firm with dominant market power. Dominant firms may be held to a higher standard under antitrust laws, due to their monopolistic potential. Small start-ups are less likely to violate competitive laws, as are buyers who are being locked in to the agreement. Exclusive dealing only pertains to vertical business partners. http://www.americanbar.org/groups/young_lawyers/publications/the_101_201_practice_ series/exclusive_dealing_an_antitrust_analysis.html
sources: SOURCE:	 Norris, L.N. (2015). Exclusive dealing: An antitrust analysis. Retrieved November 3, 2015, from

questions.question: One benefit a supplier can receive from paying a slotting allowance is
questions.explanation: Better shelf placement. Suppliers who pay slotting allowances often get better shelf placement from distributors. Lower customer demand would actually be a negative thing for suppliers. Shipping costs are unaffected by slotting allowances. Branding is the sole responsibility of the supplier and is also unaffected by slotting allowances. from http://www.wisegeek.com/what-are-slotting-allowances.htm
sources: SOURCE:	 Wisegeek.com. (2003-2015). What are slotting allowances? Retrieved November 6, 2015,

questions.question: A large online seller partners with a credit card company to leverage each other’s brand values, share marketing expertise and research, and exchange skills. The two companies are involved in
questions.explanation: Affinity marketing. Affinity marketing is a partnership between multiple businesses to provide goods in exchange for access to a new market. Affiliate marketing is the process of promoting and selling another business’s products in exchange for a sales commission. Cross linking is promoting your company on another company’s website and allowing them to promote their company on your website. Social media marketing is promoting your company over social media channels. http://affinitymarketing.co.uk/all-news/what-is-affinity-marketing/
sources: SOURCE:	 Affinity Marketing Ltd. (2015). What is affinity marketing? Retrieved November 2, 2015, from

questions.question: Sammy is going to a meeting to hear the new procedures for billing clients for completed work. One of the best ways for Sammy to follow the presenter’s oral instructions is to

options.A: take notes while the speaker is presenting the material.
options.B: take notes after listening to the meeting in entirety.
options.C: listen to the presentation and ask a coworker for notes afterwards.
options.D: listen to the presentation and attempt the task immediately afterwards.

questions.explanation: Take notes while the speaker is presenting the material. Taking notes during the presentation will help Sammy capture key points and make sure she didn’t miss any important information. Taking notes after the presentation does not ensure Sammy will retain all the necessary information. Listening to the presentation and then asking a coworker for notes or attempting the task immediately afterwards could result in missteps. indispensable at work: Follow directions. Retrieved October 23, 2015, from https://uamis. wordpress.com/2011/08/31/101-ways-to-make-yourself-indispensable-at-work-follow- directions/
sources: SOURCE:	 UA Management Information Systems. (2011, August 31). 101 ways to make yourself

questions.question: Pooja is a financial analyst taking part in a group discussion about the operations of her company. Pooja stays quiet during most of the meeting but is very vocal when the discussion focuses on how to save on operating costs. Which of the following is Pooja doing to contribute to a successful group discussion:

options.B: Only speaking if knowledgeable on the topic

questions.explanation: Only speaking if knowledgeable on the topic. Pooja remains quiet during most of the discussion but takes an active role in the financial part of the discussion because she is knowledgeable of the topic as a financial analyst. Being rigid, controlling the entire discussion, and adopting a laid back attitude are negative traits and actions that could create an unsuccessful group discussion. October 23, 2015, from http://www.managementstudyguide.com/tips-for-successful-group- discussion.htm 17
sources: SOURCE:	 Management Study Guide. (2015). Tips for a successful group discussion. Retrieved

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a good visual aid for a presentation:
questions.explanation: Supports spoken information. Good visual aids should support or complement spoken information without repeating it. They should also be simple and clear, as opposed to dense and crowded. Visual aids should supplement the presentation, but should not take over the entire presentation. October 26, 2015, from http://www.behavioralinstitute.org/uploads/giving_a_great_ presentation.pdf
sources: SOURCE:	 Behavioralinstute.org. (n.d.). Giving a great presentation: Visual aids with impact. Retrieved

questions.question: Corey is writing an online banner advertisement to sell her new line of handmade toys. What type of writing is Corey doing?
questions.explanation: Persuasive. Persuasive writing attempts to convince an audience to perform a certain action, such as buying a product. Responsive writing is used to respond to some prior communication. Informative writing is used to transfer information to an audience. Writing in the passive voice transfers the object of the action into the subject position. 2015, from http://www.directutor.com/content/effective-writing-different-message-types
sources: SOURCE:	 DirecTutor.com. (n.d.). Effective writing for different message types. Retrieved October 26,

questions.question: Lerato is participating in a staff meeting. She studied the topics before the meeting, is asking questions at the right times, and is very alert and attentive. What else should she do during the meeting?

options.A: Only speak when she agrees with others

questions.explanation: Take notes. Taking notes is one of the best ways to show engagement and will also help you retain the information from the meeting. Dominating the conversation can have a negative impact and does not help with collaboration during meetings. It is a good practice to ask many questions as long as they are appropriate and focused. Participants should contribute to the meeting even if they disagree with the content in order to create solutions or gain clarity on the subject. October 26, 2015, from http://timemanagementninja.com/2013/10/did-you-participate-in- the-meeting-or-were-you-just-there/
sources: SOURCE:	 Jarrow, C. (2013). Did you participate in the meeting or were you just there? Retrieved

questions.question: As employees go about their daily activities, their actions should be driven by whether or not they will
questions.explanation: Result in customer satisfaction. As employees go about their daily activities, their actions should be driven by whether or not they will result in customer satisfaction. Demonstrating a customer-service mindset is all about making customers feel important. By focusing on customer satisfaction, employees can potentially increase sales and benefit the business, not benefit the workforce. All employees should demonstrate a customer-service mindset regardless of whether or not they have direct contact with customers.
sources: SOURCE:	 CR LAP 4—Set Your Mind to It (Customer-Service Mindset)

questions.question: Businesses can reinforce brand promise after a sale by
questions.explanation: Resolving any problems or complaints. Businesses can reinforce brand promise after a sale by resolving any problems or complaints that may arise. Even problems can turn into positive touch points if a business handles them correctly. Creating new advertising campaigns and generating positive publicity fall into the category of pre-sale activities and touch points. Improving the company’s website can be done any time, but interaction with the site is typically a pre-sale or during-the-sale touch point.
sources: SOURCE:	 CR LAP 6—Share the Promise (Identifying Brand Promise)

questions.question: Which of the following are examples of natural resources:
questions.explanation: Land and coal. A natural resource is any resource that is found in nature and used to produce goods and services. Land, coal, and weather conditions are examples of natural resources because they are found in nature. Entrepreneurs and real estate agents are examples of human resources. Trucks are considered capital goods. 18

questions.question: Belinda runs a clothing boutique. She purchases clothing from distributors, then sells the clothing to her customers, the ultimate consumers. Which business model is Belinda using?
questions.explanation: Retailer. A retailer is a business that buys consumer goods or services and sells them to the ultimate consumer. Belinda buys clothing, then sells that clothing to the ultimate consumer. Wholesalers are intermediaries who help to move goods between producers and retailers by buying goods from producers and selling them to retailers. A manufacturer is a type of producer that changes the shapes or forms of materials so that they will be useful to consumers. This is not an example of direct sales. smallbusiness.chron.com/types-business-models-79.html
sources: SOURCE:	 Root, G.N. III. (2015). Types of business models. Retrieved November 11, 2015, from http://

questions.question: Melanie’s employer withholds certain taxes, such as local income taxes, from her paycheck. These are considered __________ taxes.
questions.explanation: Payroll. Payroll taxes are the taxes associated with being employed as well as being an employer. Employers must withhold certain taxes from their employees’ paychecks. They are not referred to as career or professional taxes. Excise taxes are built into the purchase price of products.
sources: SOURCE:	 EC LAP 27—Pay Your Share (Business Taxes)

questions.question: A small startup company decides to specialize its labor force. What is an advantage of this form of specialization?
questions.explanation: Simplified worker training. Simplified worker training is an advantage of specialization by stage of production or tasks because employees are able to focus on one piece of the total job instead of learning multiple tasks. Worker dependency is a disadvantage of specialization because employees need to rely on each other to complete a task. Production rates normally increase through specialization, as does the quality of work.
sources: SOURCE:	 EC LAP 7—Divide and Conquer (Specialization and Division of Labor)

questions.question: Country A places a ban on importing pork from Country B because it believes the pork may be tainted. This is considered a trade
questions.explanation: Barrier. Trade barriers are restrictions placed on trade between countries. If one country will not import pork from another country, it is enacting a trade barrier. This is not referred to as a trade tax, toll, or dependence. http://www.investopedia.com/articles/economics/08/tariff-trade-barrier-basics.asp
sources: SOURCE:	 Radcliffe, B. (2015). The basics of tariffs and trade barriers. Retrieved December 1, 2015, from

questions.question: Entrepreneurs contribute to our society by
questions.explanation: Creating new jobs. Entrepreneurs contribute to society by providing jobs for employees. They do not have to donate to charities, run for political office, or make friends with employees to contribute to society. Glencoe/McGraw-Hill.
sources: SOURCE:	 Farese, L.S., Kimbrell, G., Woloszyk, C.A. (2012). Marketing essentials (p. 779). Columbus, OH:

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of ethical work behavior:

options.A: Cynthia takes credit for her coworker’s idea.
options.B: Wyatt spends most of his work day shopping for shoes online.
options.C: Demetrius is honest and admits making a mistake.
options.D: Ria decides not to finish the project her boss assigned her.

questions.explanation: Demetrius is honest and admits making a mistake. Ethical employees are responsible workers who adhere to a standard of appropriate employee behavior. When Demetrius is honest and admits making a mistake, he is behaving ethically. Being ethical means being accountable for your actions and admitting your mistakes rather than trying to cover them up or blame others. Ethical employees do not spend work time shopping online, take credit for their coworkers’ ideas, or avoid finishing projects. 19
sources: SOURCE:	 EI LAP 4—Work Right (Ethical Work Habits)

questions.question: Religion, work experience, and education level are all considered __________ dimensions of diversity.
questions.explanation: Secondary. Secondary dimensions of diversity are those characteristics that may change at various points throughout one’s lifetime. They include such characteristics as religion, work experience, education level, income level, language, and family status. Primary dimensions are those inborn differences that cannot be changed and affect a person’s entire life. Ethnocentrism is the belief that your own culture is naturally better than other cultures. Collectivist cultures are geared toward group accomplishments.
sources: SOURCE:	 EI LAP 11—Getting to Know You (Cultural Sensitivity)

questions.question: You think you deserve more paid vacation time, but you have to convince your boss to agree with you. Which of the following things should you do if you want to sell your boss on your idea:

options.A: Listen carefully to your boss so you can address her objections.
options.B: Avoid practicing what you say so it will sound more natural.
options.C: Send your boss an email, instead of talking to her in person.
options.D: Approach your boss on a day when she is busy. 3

questions.explanation: Listen carefully to your boss so you can address her objections. When attempting to sell someone an idea, you should listen more than you talk. Listening to your boss will let you know what her objections are, which will make it easier for you to address them. You should practice what you will say to your boss and speak to her in person, not via email. Approach your boss when she is not busy. 11, 2015, from http://smallbusiness.chron.com/types-business-models-79.html
sources: SOURCE:	 Fisher, A. (2013, December 20). 10 ways to sell your best ideas at work. Retrieved November

questions.question: What can you do to show nonverbal assertiveness?
questions.explanation: Look people in the eye. When you are trying to be assertive, it is important to use the right body language to reinforce your message. Your body language is a nonverbal way of being assertive. Looking other people in the eye when you speak to them is one way of being nonverbally assertive. Starting conversations, asking questions, and requesting help are all ways of showing verbal assertiveness.

questions.question: Consensus can only be reached if all group members
questions.explanation: Agree. Consensus can only be reached when each group member agrees. Group members should not give up when attempting to reach consensus. The consensus-building process may introduce conflict, but this doesn’t mean that group members need to argue. Group members do not need to be friends to reach consensus.
sources: SOURCE:	 EI LAP 19—It’s a Group Thing (Consensus Building)

questions.question: One way to establish credibility is to be
questions.explanation: Reliable. Good leaders have credibility. The best ways to establish credibility are to display competence and reliability (dependability). A good leader may also be creative, happy, and flexible, but these are not necessarily ways to establish credibility.
sources: SOURCE:	 EI LAP 16—Lead the Way (Concept of Leadership)

questions.question: Glenn is investing $1,000 today and must decide which investment will make him the most money in the next 10 years. Which investment should he choose?

options.A: An investment that receives 5% interest compounded annually
options.B: An investment that receives 5% interest compounded semi-annually
options.C: An investment that receives 5% simple interest semi-annually
options.D: An investment that receives 10% simple interest annually

questions.explanation: An investment that receives 5% interest compounded semi-annually. An investment that is compounded at 5% semi-annually will accumulate more interest than an investment that receives 5% compounded annually, 5% simple interest semi-annually, or even 10% simple interest annually. investopedia.com/terms/c/compoundinterest.asp 20
sources: SOURCE:	 Investopedia. (2015). Compound interest. Retrieved October 28, 2015, from http://www.

questions.question: For budgeting purposes, which of the following would be considered a long-term financial goal:
questions.explanation: Saving for retirement. Long-term goals, such as saving for retirement, take longer than a couple of years to achieve. Buying a used car, saving for an annual vacation, and creating an emergency fund should all take less than two years and are known as short-term financial goals. Retrieved October 27, 2015, from http://www.capitalone.com/financial-education/savings- and-investing/savings/short-and-long-term-goals/
sources: SOURCE:	 Capital One. (2015). Savings basics - Setting short-term and long-term savings goals.

questions.question: One reason that the balance on your bank statement would be higher than your checkbook register would be that

options.A: you’ve written checks after the covered period.
options.B: a deposit has not been credited to your account yet.
options.C: an automated payment was deducted from your account.
options.D: you did not record a bank withdrawal.

questions.explanation: You’ve written checks after the covered period. Your bank statement’s balance would be higher than your checkbook register if you’ve written checks after the covered period. An uncredited account, an automatic bill deduction, or an unrecorded bank withdrawal would not make your bank statement higher. Instead, those things may make it lower than your checkbook register. from http://www.bankrate.com/finance/checking/anatomy-of-a-bank-account-statement-1. aspx
sources: SOURCE:	 Bankrate.com. (2015). Anatomy of a bank account statement. Retrieved October 27, 2015,

questions.question: What type of legislation requires businesses to make relevant financial information available to investors?
questions.explanation: Disclosure laws. Disclosure laws are designed to protect investors by requiring businesses to disclose financial information that could be considered relevant to making an investment decision. Antitrust laws are regulations that prevent a person or company from taking any actions to restrain free trade and competition in the marketplace. Intellectual property laws protect intangible business assets resulting from thinking processes. Testimonials are statements by users of a product, proclaiming the benefits received from the use of that product. from http://www.referenceforbusiness.com/encyclopedia/Dev-Eco/Disclosure-Laws-and- Regulations.html
sources: SOURCE:	 Reference for Business. (2015). Disclosure laws and regulations. Retrieved October 26, 2015,

questions.question: What is the relationship between data and information?

options.A: Information cannot become data unless it is presented in a report.
options.B: Information becomes data after being processed and organized.
options.C: Data and information are the same thing.
options.D: Data become information after being processed and organized.

questions.explanation: Data become information after being processed and organized. Data are facts and figures. Information, on the other hand, is data presented in a useful form. For data to become information, they must be processed, organized, and presented in an understandable, meaningful manner. Data and information are not the same thing.
sources: SOURCE:	 IM LAP 2—Get the Facts Straight (Marketing-Information Management)

questions.question: Which of the following things should a marketer do before beginning an email campaign?

options.A: Buy email lists from other companies.
options.B: Make sure recipients cannot opt out of the emails.
options.D: Prepare to send emails multiple times a day. 4

questions.explanation: Research email regulations and etiquette. It’s important for marketers to research email regulations and etiquette before beginning an email campaign. It is required that recipients be able to opt out of promotional emails. Companies should not buy email lists. If a company sends multiple emails a day, it runs the risk of annoying its customers. them. Retrieved November 6, 2015, from http://blog.hubspot.com/insiders/email-marketing- compliance
sources: SOURCE:	 Acker, S. (2014, June 12). Your email marketing tactics might be illegal. Here’s how to fix

questions.question: When conducting marketing research, what is the first step of the process?

options.D: Identify the reason for the research

questions.explanation: Identify the reason for the research. The first step in the research process is identifying the problem, issue, situation, or concern to be researched. This is an important step because if the business does not clearly identify and define the reason for the research, the business may invest its time and money unwisely. Developing a hypothesis, collecting the needed data, and making recommendations based on findings all come later in the research process. 21
sources: SOURCE:	 IM LAP 5—Seek and Find (Marketing Research)

questions.question: A company’s managers ask themselves, “Why is our market share dropping?” This is an example of a __________-oriented decision problem.
questions.explanation: Discovery. Discovery-oriented decision problems are aimed at answering the questions “what?” or “why?” Strategy-oriented questions are aimed at answering the questions “how?” or “which?” A research problem asks what research needs to be done to solve the decision problem. This is not referred to as a situation-oriented problem.
sources: SOURCE:	 IM LAP 13—What’s the Problem? (Marketing-Research Problems)

questions.question: A team of marketers want to increase the company’s sales, but they don’t know why sales are low and they aren’t sure which direction to go in. The marketers should conduct __________ research.
questions.explanation: Exploratory. Marketing research that collects information to help the business define its issue, situation, or concern and decide the direction in which to go to address it is called exploratory research. In other words, marketers often use exploratory research when they don’t have much to go on. Descriptive research is conducted only after marketers have a good understanding of the question or problem they’re dealing with. Causal research is marketing research that focuses on cause and effect and tests “what if?” theories. Test marketing is an example of causal research.
sources: SOURCE:	 IM LAP 14—Better By Design (Marketing-Research Designs)

questions.question: Mara’s group needs to decide who will be the group leader, so each member writes his/her name on a piece of paper. Then all the pieces of paper are put in a hat and one name is drawn. What method did Mara’s group use to pick its leader?
questions.explanation: Simple random sampling. Simple random sampling gives every sampling unit a known and equal chance of being selected. Systematic random sampling is similar to simple random sampling, but it requires that the sampling units be put into list form. With stratified random sampling, marketers break their target population down into non-overlapping groups (called strata) and then select simple random samples from each group (stratum). When the sample size from each stratum is dependent on that stratum’s size relative to the entire population, it’s known as proportionally stratified sampling.
sources: SOURCE:	 IM LAP 16—Take Your Pick (Nature of Sampling Plans)

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of the tracking method of data collection:
questions.explanation: Automation. Automation is an advantage of the tracking method of data collection. Tracking allows researchers to obtain, record, and organize massive amounts of data without humans having to do all the work. Tracking does not stimulate in-depth discussion, and it does not give researchers the ability to observe nonverbal cues. Privacy concerns are a disadvantage of tracking.
sources: SOURCE:	 IM LAP 17—Hunting and Gathering (Data-Collection Methods)

questions.question: It is important to make sure that survey questions

options.C: are well written and easily understood.

questions.explanation: Are well written and easily understood. Survey questions should be well written and easily understood. They should be clearly written so that they will not be interpreted multiple ways. They should be objective and not encourage a preferred answer. Each question should address only one concern. Columbus, OH: Glencoe/McGraw-Hill.
sources: SOURCE:	 Farese, L.S., Kimbrell, G., & Woloszyk, C.A. (2012). Marketing essentials (pp. 689-693).

questions.question: What is one way to monitor the buzz your company receives?

options.A: Update your company’s website to make it easier to use and find.
options.B: Have social media conversations with interested customers about your products.
options.C: Answer customer complaints promptly on social media.
options.D: Use website analytics to see which social media sites customers use to find your website.

questions.explanation: Use website analytics to see which social media sites customers use to find your website. Website analytics can help you determine how many people are visiting your website and which sites they’re using to find your website. This can be helpful in measuring how effective your various social media presences are in drawing in customers. Talking to customers on social media, answering customer complaints, and updating your website are important, but they do not measure buzz. November 11, 2015, from http://greenbuzzagency.com/tips-for-measuring-your-video- marketing-success/ 22
sources: SOURCE:	 Green Buzz Agency. (2015). Tips for measuring your video marketing strategy. Retrieved

questions.question: Over the course of the semester, Rita gets the following scores on her marketing exams: 85, 92, 96, 92, 88, 95, and 100. What is the mode in this set of test scores?
questions.explanation: 92. The mode is the number that occurs the most frequently. 92 is the only number that occurs more than once, so it is the mode in this set of numbers. OH: Glencoe/McGraw-Hill.
sources: SOURCE:	 Farese, L.S., Kimbrell, G., & Woloszyk, C.A. (2012). Marketing essentials (p. 183). Columbus,

questions.question: Angela is conducting a research study on whether too much television harms children. Angela believes that it does, and she asks the participants leading questions to support her belief. Angela’s study will not be accurate because of
questions.explanation: Researcher bias. Angela is biased because she is allowing her personal beliefs to affect the study. This means that her results will not be accurate, because they were designed to reflect her personal beliefs. There is nothing in this example to indicate that Angela’s study suffers from financial constraints, measuring errors, or processing errors. November 11, 2015, from http://blog.cruxresearch.com/2013/08/27/the-top-5-errors-and- biases-in-survey-research/
sources: SOURCE:	 Crux Research. (2013, August 27). The top 5 errors and biases in survey research. Retrieved

questions.question: Which of the following does an effective marketing questionnaire include:

options.D: At least five pages of questions.

questions.explanation: Clear instructions at the beginning. Effective marketing questionnaires include clear instructions. Questionnaires should be brief (about one or two pages, not five). Questions should be phrased objectively and address one issue at a time. November 11, 2015, from http://www.allbusiness.com/effective-questionnaires-for-market- research-1959-1.html
sources: SOURCE:	 All Business. (1999-2015). Effective questionnaires for marketing research. Retrieved

questions.question: Why should marketing researchers use more than one method to acquire data?

options.A: Because customers may not like certain methods of marketing research
options.B: Because multiple methods will give a more accurate picture of customer behavior
options.C: Because certain methods of marketing research are known for being inaccurate
options.D: Because individual marketers may not like certain methods of marketing research

questions.explanation: Because multiple methods will give a more accurate picture of customer behavior. If marketing researchers want to have the most accurate results possible, it is wise to use multiple methods of marketing research. If a certain method of marketing research is known for being inaccurate, then researchers should not use that method. Customers or researchers not liking a certain method of marketing research does not necessarily affect its accuracy. http://www.buzzle.com/articles/market-research-methods.html
sources: SOURCE:	 Godbole, M. (2010, April 2). Market research methods. Retrieved November 11, 2015, from

questions.question: A dry cleaner assures a customer that his clothes will be ready on Tuesday. However, when the customer shows up on Tuesday, the clothes haven’t been cleaned. The dry cleaner is demonstrating which action that companies should avoid?
questions.explanation: Not keeping promises. Customers aren’t able to trust a business that doesn’t keep promises. In business, a good name means everything. Your customers and employees must be assured that you will do what you say you will do. Using high pressure sales techniques, responding inappropriately to crises, and acting unethically are all negative company actions, but they are not illustrated in this example.
sources: SOURCE:	 MK LAP 3—Just Do It…Right (Company Actions and Results)

questions.question: An apparel company separates its customers into north, south, west, and east segments and markets to each group based on what the weather is like in that part of the country. The company is using __________ segmentation to group its customers.
questions.explanation: Geographic. Grouping customers according to where they are located is geographic segmentation. Customers are grouped on the basis of such geographic divisions as regions, states, cities, and neighborhoods. Demographic segmentation divides a market on the basis of its physical and social characteristics. Lifestyles and personalities of customers are used as the basis for psychographic segmentation of markets. Dividing customers into groups according to their response to a product is behavioral segmentation. 23
sources: SOURCE:	 MP LAP 3—Have We Met? (Market Identification)

questions.question: SWOT analysis is an important part of the __________ process.
questions.explanation: Marketing planning. The SWOT analysis assesses a company’s strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats. It is an important part of the marketing planning process. It is not part of the hiring, staff training, or inventory buying process. Glencoe/McGraw-Hill.
sources: SOURCE:	 Farese, L.S., Kimbrell, G., Woloszyk, C.A. (2012). Marketing essentials (p. 31). Columbus, OH:

questions.question: A company regularly investigates its competitors, including what products the competitors make. The company also investigates economic factors and new technology and looks at how these things will affect the company. The company is conducting __________ scanning.
questions.explanation: Environmental. Environmental scanning is collecting information about the environment surrounding your business. This can include information about competitors, the economy, and technology. This is not referred to as technological, ecological, or organizational scanning. Retrieved November 11, 2015, from http://guides.matc.edu/marketingplan
sources: SOURCE:	 Bythell, B. (2015, November 9). Resources for creating a marketing plan: Environmental scan.

questions.question: How would the introduction of a highly anticipated new product likely affect a business’s sales forecast?

options.A: It would be impossible to predict sales.
options.B: Predicted sales would be extremely low.
options.C: Predicted sales would vary based on geographic location.

questions.explanation: Predicted sales would be high. When a business introduces a highly anticipated new product (e.g., the latest version of the iPhone), predicted sales are usually high, not low. It is quite possible to predict sales for new products. Only in rare cases would geographic location have an impact on such a product.
sources: SOURCE:	 MP LAP 5—Futurecast (Nature of Sales Forecasts)

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of an article that contains valid information:

options.A: It has citations and references to reputable journals.
options.B: It is published on a very popular blog.
options.C: It is part of a persuasive political pamphlet.
options.D: It was written at least ten years ago.

questions.explanation: It has citations and references to reputable journals. If an article is valid, it should cite its sources and include references to other valid studies, articles, or books. Being published on a popular blog does not necessarily mean an article contains valid information. If an article is in a persuasive political pamphlet, it may be biased, which means that the information will not necessarily be valid. If an article was written at least ten years ago, it may not be timely, which could affect its validity. November 11, 2015, from http://www.ehow.com/how_5035804_determine-validity-research- article.html
sources: SOURCE:	 Cooper, M. (1999-2015). How to determine the validity of a research article. Retrieved

questions.question: A business keeps a lot of customer information on file. In order to collect and store this information ethically, the business should be sure that the information

options.A: is kept on a public computer.
options.D: is shared with everyone in the company.

questions.explanation: Contains only necessary data. A business should only store the customer data and information that it needs. The information stored should not contain subjective language. It should be kept on a computer that is only accessible by people who need the information, and it should not be shared with everyone in the company. Information management. Retrieved December 3, 2015, from http://www.aasw.asn.au/ document/item/4680
sources: SOURCE:	 Australian Association of Social Workers. (2015, December). Ethics and practice guideline-

questions.question: Windows, OSX, and Linux are examples of
questions.explanation: Operating systems. Operating systems are software that allow computer hard drives to communicate with computer software. Windows, OSX, and Linux are three examples of operating systems. They are not examples of computer wallpaper, Internet browsers, or word-processing programs. computerhope.com/os.htm 24
sources: SOURCE:	 Computer Hope. (2015). Operating systems. Retrieved November 11, 2015, from http://www.

questions.question: If you used a query in a database, you would most likely want to

options.A: view your requested data in one datasheet.
options.C: create a new row in a table.
options.D: input new information into the database.

questions.explanation: View your requested data in one datasheet. Queries allow you to retrieve specific data and view it in one datasheet. This is helpful if you need to view certain data that are spread out throughout your database. Queries do not close the database, create a new row in a table, or input new information into the database. com/en-za/article/Database-basics-a849ac16-07c7-4a31-9948-3c8c94a7c204#bmqueries
sources: SOURCE:	 Office. (2015). Database basics. Retrieved December 8, 2015, from https://support.office.

questions.question: You have a large amount of customer data at your office, and you want to sort through the information and find patterns. You should use data
questions.explanation: Mining. Data mining involves processing information and finding patterns. If you want to find patterns in your customer data, you should use data mining, not data elimination, entry, or creation. 1, 2015, from http://www.buzzle.com/articles/advantages-of-data-mining.html
sources: SOURCE:	 Buzzle. (2011, October 4). Advantages of data mining in different fields. Retrieved December

questions.question: The C Corp, an airplane parts producer, is expanding its operations to achieve efficiency through mass production. What technology would best help the company expand and streamline its operations?
questions.explanation: Computer-aided manufacturing (CAM). CAM is a process in which computers are used to collect and store data and to instruct automated manufacturing equipment in the production process. A technical data management system (TDMS) is a document management system dealing with technical/ engineering documents. An electronic data interchange (EDI) is used to send information from one company to another electronically. Material requirements planning (MRP) is a production planning and inventory control system. http://www.plm.automation.siemens.com/en_us/plm/cam.shtml
sources: SOURCE:	 Siemens. (2015). CAM / Computer-Aided Manufacturing. Retrieved October 28, 2015, from

questions.question: Chandra is in charge of data security in a large corporation. To make sure the data security is effective, Chandra should hire a third party to conduct a yearly
questions.explanation: Audit. A yearly audit by a third party is good practice to make sure business rules are being implemented properly by all team members. A third party will ensure unbiased results. Encryption standards should be implemented, but it isn’t necessary to have a third party inspect the encryption tools unless there is a known breach. Backups of secure information should be done daily or as often as possible. Employee background checks are customary for new hires but don’t make sense financially to do yearly. environment. Retrieved October 28, 2015, from http://www.blackstratus.com/blog/practices- maintaining-data-security-business-environment/
sources: SOURCE:	 Murphy, R. (2014, June 9). 17 best practices for maintaining data security in a business

questions.question: You’ve just come up with a great tagline for your company and want to use it on all marketing materials. Before publishing the new tagline, you should get it
questions.explanation: Trademarked. A trademark is a symbol, design, or word used to identify a good or service and registered with the government to prevent use by others. A patent is the legal protection of an invention or a process granted by government to its owners for a certain number of years. Notarizing authenticates a document legally (normally used for titles, affidavits, depositions, etc.). Audits are used to verify a company’s accounts, records, and financial data. http://www.uspto.gov/trademarks-getting-started/trademark-basics/trademark-patent-or- copyright 25
sources: SOURCE:	 USPTO. (2011-2013). Trademark, patent, or copyright? Retrieved October 28, 2015, from

questions.question: Jack is measuring how well the purchasing department is performing against the department’s budget. Jack is measuring the __________ of the department.
questions.explanation: Purchasing efficiency. Purchasing efficiency is a performance measurement that relates to how well the purchasing department is performing against the budget that is in place for the department. Purchasing functionality is a performance measurement that measures the department against the functional requirements to provide the correct item at the right time at the lowest cost. Supplier relationships are not a performance measurement, although good supplier relationships should increase performance. Purchasing improvements would be measured from past measurements. http://logistics.about.com/od/strategicsupplychain/a/measure_purchasing.htm
sources: SOURCE:	 Murray, M. (2015). Measuring purchasing performance. Retrieved October 27, 2015, from

questions.question: One of the major reasons that companies strive to have the most optimized supply chain is to
questions.explanation: Lower costs. Optimized supply chains lower costs by eliminating unnecessary transactions and suppliers. Increasing suppliers and increasing inventory are not goals linked to an optimized supply chain and would most likely have a negative effect. Supply chain does not attempt to eliminate defects; instead, it tries to streamline processes and lower costs for the company. investopedia.com/terms/s/supplychain.asp
sources: SOURCE:	 Investopedia. (2015). Supply chain. Retrieved October 29, 2015, from http://www.

questions.question: You want to earn a raise at work. This is a(n) __________ goal.
questions.explanation: Performance. A performance goal is described by the level of performance reached or by a task completed. A learning goal has to do with mastering a new skill. This goal is not necessarily unrealistic or easy.
sources: SOURCE:	 PD LAP 16—Go for the Goal (Goal Setting)

questions.question: When Carmen faces a challenge at work, she finds a way to turn it into an opportunity. Carmen has __________ skills.
questions.explanation: Problem-solving. If Carmen is able to turn her challenges into opportunities, she has problem- solving skills. Carmen can find her way over whatever obstacle she is facing. Problem-solving skills are important in the workplace because they can help you achieve your professional goals. Communication, time-management, and teamwork skills are all important in the workplace, but Carmen is not illustrating them in this example.
sources: SOURCE:	 PD LAP 77—No Problem (Demonstrating Problem-Solving Skills)

questions.question: A business is much more likely to fail if its owner starts it simply to

options.C: make a difference in others’ lives.

questions.explanation: Make money. Many people start businesses to supplement their existing income or gain financial independence. However, a business is much more likely to fail if its owner starts it simply to make money. Money should never be your top priority in starting a new business. If it is, you are much more likely to make hasty, unwise decisions that may help to make money in the short run but will ultimately destroy your chance for success. Obtaining personal satisfaction, making a difference in the lives of others, and becoming more independent are all reasons that entrepreneurs start businesses.
sources: SOURCE:	 PD LAP 66—Own Your Own (Career Opportunities in Entrepreneurship)

questions.question: Finn just started a new job, and he wants to be sure that he makes a good impression on his boss and coworkers. When he gets sick during his second week on the job, what should he do?

options.A: Avoid going to work or bothering his boss with a phone call.
options.B: Go to work no matter what, even if he’s contagious.
options.C: Follow company attendance and sick leave policies.
options.D: Assume that his boss will fire him if he misses a day.

questions.explanation: Follow company attendance and sick leave policies. It’s important for employees to follow their company’s attendance and sick leave policies when taking time off of work for an illness. Finn should not necessarily go to work no matter what or assume that his boss will fire him. Finn should notify his boss, in a way that’s outlined in his company’s policies, that he will be missing work. from http://smallbusiness.chron.com/examples-company-policies-procedures-10995.html 26
sources: SOURCE:	 Scott, S. (2015). Examples of company policies and procedures. Retrieved December 8, 2015,

questions.question: Tania’s main job duties include organizing focus groups, product tests, and surveys. Tania likely works in
questions.explanation: Marketing research. Marketing researchers are in charge of figuring out what customers need and want and why they do what they do. Marketing research jobs focus on marketing activities that involve determining information needs, designing data-collection processes, collecting data, analyzing data, presenting data, organizing focus groups, product tests, and surveys, and using data for marketing planning. Customer service, sales, and public relations professionals do not organize focus groups, product tests, and surveys.
sources: SOURCE:	 PD LAP 21—Career Opportunities in Marketing

questions.question: Sharif is deciding whether he should advertise his product on the radio, in the newspaper, or in a television ad. He is making a __________ decision.
questions.explanation: Promotion. Promotion involves the various types of communications that marketers use to inform, persuade, or remind customers of their products. When Sharif decides between advertising on the radio, in the newspaper, or in a television ad, he’s making a promotion decision. Pricing is a marketing function that involves the determination of an exchange price at which the buyer and seller perceive optimum value for a good or service. Place involves shipping, handling, and storing products and determining when and where they will be available. Product decisions involve determining what goods, services, or ideas to produce or sell that will satisfy customers’ needs and wants.
sources: SOURCE:	 PI LAP 2—The Price Is Right (Nature of Pricing)

questions.question: One of smaller online retailers’ biggest concerns about automated pricing systems is that these systems could cause

options.C: the retailer’s price to be undercut.

questions.explanation: The retailer’s price to be undercut. Automated pricing systems allow large retailers to price products aggressively and undercut smaller retailers. Automated pricing systems do not cause prices to go up or result in a surplus of demand. They also have no connection with marketing prices for the retailer. retail business. Retrieved November 6, 2015, from http://upstreamcommerce.com/ blog/2011/10/10/3-ways-automated-pricing-systems-harm-online-retail-business
sources: SOURCE:	 Miller, G. (October 10, 2011). 3 ways automated pricing systems can harm your online

questions.question: X Corporation is the dominant electronic manufacturer in the world. To drive out smaller competitors, the company plans to lower its prices to well below the market average. The X Corporation is likely to

options.C: keep prices down as a monopoly.

questions.explanation: Be charged with predatory pricing. Predatory pricing is typically prohibited under national competition laws and involves temporarily pricing products below market value to end competitive threats and monopolize the market. False advertising is a promotion that deceives customers. If X Corporation succeeds in driving out competition, it will most likely raise the prices of its products above market value. Price discrimination occurs when a business charges different customers different prices for similar amounts and types of products. pedia.com/predatory+pricing
sources: SOURCE:	 Maxi-pedia. (2015). Predatory pricing. Retrieved November 10, 2015, from http://www.maxi-

questions.question: The Sweets Company sells sugar, which is a commodity product. Most likely, the company has
questions.explanation: Minimal pricing flexibility. In a commodity market, there are many competitors with non-unique products, which creates minimal pricing flexibility over pricing for any producer. An uncontrolled market would allow for maximum pricing flexibility. A controlled market would allow for moderate pricing flexibility. A commodity market would never have complete control over market pricing because there are too many competitors and the product is not unique. from http://www.businessplans.org/Pricing.html 27
sources: SOURCE:	 Business Resource Software, Inc. (n.d.). Issues affecting price. Retrieved November 10, 2015,

questions.question: A fast-food company creates a new sandwich. It decides to market the sandwich as a healthier option for busy customers. What part of product/service management is this company participating in?
questions.explanation: Positioning. Positioning is a strategy in which a business creates a certain image or impression of a product in the minds of consumers. Test marketing involves introducing the product to a limited market to see how it will be accepted. Licensing means buying permission from another person or company to sell a product. Commercialization is the point at which the product goes into full-scale production, a marketing plan is put in place, service and sales training are conducted, and the product’s life cycle begins.
sources: SOURCE:	 PM LAP 17—Rapping Up Products (Nature of Product/Service Management)

questions.question: During the decline stage, a company may choose to discontinue a product and sell off the remaining inventory in a process known as
questions.explanation: Liquidation. During the decline stage, a company may choose to discontinue a product and sell off the remaining inventory in a process known as liquidation (converting assets to cash). Penetration pricing is a strategy used during the introduction stage, where marketers price a product low to achieve rapid market acceptance. A company may or may not sell inventory in a super sale—they often sell it to another company, not retail customers. A foreclosure occurs when a person or business can no longer make payments on a piece of property.
sources: SOURCE:	 PM LAP 18—Get a Life (Cycle) (Impact of Product Life Cycles)

questions.question: A snack food manufacturer provides a grocery store with a computer-developed diagram that shows how all of the food products should be displayed in the store. The manufacturer is providing a
questions.explanation: Planogram. A planogram is a computer-developed diagram that instructs retailers on how to display products. This is not referred to as a product diagram. Product placement is a type of media that uses products as props in movies, television shows, and computer games. A prototype is an early sample or model of a product. Columbus, OH: Glencoe/McGraw-Hill.
sources: SOURCE:	 Farese, L.S., Kimbrell, G., & Woloszyk, C.A. (2012). Marketing essentials (pp. 721-723).

questions.question: Brittany wants to open a restaurant. She notices that there are no gourmet burger restaurants in her city, so that’s what she decides to focus on. Because there are no other restaurants like hers in the city, she is able to make a profit. Brittany identified a product __________ gap.
questions.explanation: Opportunity. A product opportunity gap occurs when there is a hole in the market that a business can fill. Brittany sees that there aren’t any gourmet burger restaurants, so she is able to use that hole in the market to create a successful business. An opportunity gap is not referred to as a problem gap, a challenge gap, or a deficit gap. http://www.celebritybrandingagency.com/jwdicks/the-opportunity-gap.php
sources: SOURCE:	 Celebrity Branding Agency. (2015). The opportunity gap. Retrieved November 19, 2015, from

questions.question: A company is trying to generate ideas for new products, so it assembles all of its staff members and has everyone give their ideas. Which method of idea generation is this?
questions.explanation: Brainstorming. Brainstorming is a creative-thinking technique involving the identification of as many different ideas as possible during a certain time frame. It is most often used in a group setting. When a company gets all of its employees together to generate ideas, it is using brainstorming. This is not an example of observation, trend research, or customers as a method of idea generation. [p. 314]. Tinley Park, IL: The Goodheart-Willcox Co.
sources: SOURCE:	 Clark, B., Basteri, C.G., Gassen, C., & Walker, M. (2014). Marketing dynamics (3rd ed.)

questions.question: Who is generally responsible for assigning grades to products like food?
questions.explanation: Government agencies. Government agencies are generally responsible for grading. For example, many of the foods you eat have been graded by the government. People who purchase the product, online review sites, and university researchers do not assign grades to food. 28
sources: SOURCE:	 PM LAP 8—Raise the Bar (Grades and Standards)

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit that businesses receive when they protect consumers:
questions.explanation: Customer loyalty. When businesses protect consumers, they project a positive image of social responsibility which often increases customer loyalty. Increased customer loyalty can also increase profits, but it doesn’t mean the profits will be record-breaking. Consumer protection will not reduce a business’s competition or lower its taxes.
sources: SOURCE:	 PM LAP 7—Protect and Serve (Consumer Protection)

questions.question: A company decides to stop manufacturing one of its products because the necessary raw materials have become too difficult to find and purchase. The company is using a(n) __________ product-mix strategy.
questions.explanation: Contraction. Contraction means removing product items or lines from the product mix. A business might decide to delete a product from its mix because its production has become a problem. Alteration involves making changes in the company’s products or lines. Expansion occurs when a business expands its product mix by adding additional product items or lines. When a company decides to add a higher priced product or line to its mix, it is using a trading-up strategy.
sources: SOURCE:	 PM LAP 3—Mix & Match (Nature of the Product Mix)

questions.question: Builderwebs is an online startup that builds websites for businesses for $100 per web page. For just $10 more, the company will also optimize the web page for search engines. Which tactic is Builderwebs using to cross-sell to its customers?
questions.explanation: Product bundling. Product bundling is a marketing strategy that involves offering several products for sale as one combined product. Cross linking is a method of adding links on your website to other websites with similar content or interests. Customer loyalty programs are put in place to reward customers for repeat business. Content marketing is the creation and distribution of content to attract customer engagement. http://www.wisegeek.com/what-is-product-bundling.htm
sources: SOURCE:	 Wisegeek. (2003-2015). What is product bundling? Retrieved November 12, 2015, from

questions.question: Marketers can use the element of price to help with positioning by

options.B: choosing a price the target market is willing to pay.
options.C: pricing products as low as possible.
options.D: pricing products too high to make them seem like luxury items.

questions.explanation: Choosing a price the target market is willing to pay. Marketers can use the element of price to help with positioning by choosing a price the target market is willing to pay. Pricing products too high will reduce sales, but pricing products as low as possible will not always support the product’s position. Allowing retailers to set prices may not support the product’s position, either.
sources: SOURCE:	 PM LAP 19—Getting Piece of Mind (Factors Used to Position Products/Services)

questions.question: Jeremy’s Nutrition Company brands its new J-Bar protein bar with a logo, tagline, and color scheme. What is the final stage of brand loyalty that new products like the J-Bar strive to reach?
questions.explanation: Brand insistence. Brand insistence is the final stage of brand loyalty in which consumers insist upon buying a specific brand. Brand awareness is one of the first stages of branding and is the extent to which customers or potential customers are familiar with a brand. Brand preference comes before insistence and is the stage of brand loyalty in which consumers prefer to purchase a certain brand but will accept substitutes if the brand is not available. Brand recognition is the stage of brand loyalty in which consumers are made aware of a brand’s existence. November 13, 2015, from http://aaron.com/2012/02/06/building-your-brand-%E2%80%93- number-4-%E2%80%93-brand-insistence/
sources: SOURCE:	 The Raymond Aaron Group. (2015). Building your brand-Brand insistence. Retrieved

questions.question: Your company is marketing a new chocolate bar. Which touchpoint do you think would be most important for your brand?
questions.explanation: Taste. For many consumables, such as chocolate bars, taste is typically the most important touchpoint. Although packaging, online advertising, and email campaigns are important touchpoints, the taste of a chocolate bar is most likely to be more important than any marketing efforts. Even with good marketing, a chocolate bar that tastes awful will probably not sell. sbinfocanada.about.com/od/marketing/a/brandbuildingsg.htm 29
sources: SOURCE:	 Glatstein, S. (2015). 5 steps in brand building. Retrieved November 12, 2015, from http://

questions.question: To effectively position a brand in the marketplace, a company should first decide what __________ makes it stand out from competition.
questions.explanation: Unique selling proposition. A company’s unique selling proposition is one of the most important factors for a brand’s success and differentiates it from competition. Although packaging, marketing tactics, and advertising platforms can help a brand stand out in the marketplace, these are only secondary to the unique selling proposition of a brand. businessarticles.org/product-positioning.htm
sources: SOURCE:	 Levis, D. (2005-2015). Product positioning. Retrieved November 12, 2015, from http://www.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of promotion:

options.B: Promotion encourages a lower standard of living.
options.D: Promotion can create and reinforce stereotypes.

questions.explanation: Promotion supports mass media. The mass communication media—radio, television, newspapers, and magazines—are either partially or completely supported by promotional dollars. Without this money, financial support of the media would probably be passed along to customers. Promotion encourages a higher standard of living. Promotion can sometimes be deceptive or create and reinforce stereotypes, but these are disadvantages, not advantages.
sources: SOURCE:	 PR LAP 4—Know Your Options (Types of Promotion)

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of stealth marketing:

options.B: An advertisement disguised as an editorial
options.D: Customer reviews not paid for by a company

questions.explanation: An advertisement disguised as an editorial. Many people view stealth marketing as unethical, especially in cases where ads are made to look like unbiased articles or editorials. Online banner advertisements, search engine optimization, and legitimate customer reviews are not instances of stealth marketing because they are not done secretly. http://www.wisegeek.com/what-is-stealth-marketing.htm
sources: SOURCE:	 Wisegeek. (2003-2015). What is stealth marketing? Retrieved November 17, 2015, from

questions.question: Sarah’s C-Shells is a small retailer that sells seashell jewelry online. After using all of its capital for inventory and the online store, the company has no funds for promotion. Which promotional method would you recommend the company use?
questions.explanation: Social media posts. Social media posts are an inexpensive way to reach a targeted audience. Interactive banner ads can be expensive with development costs. Television commercials and radio broadcasts can be expensive on a national level. Cheaper television commercials and radio broadcasts usually only target a small geographic location, which may not be effective for an online retailer. boundless.com/marketing/textbooks/boundless-marketing-textbook/integrated-marketing- communications-12/the-promotion-mix-83/social-media-417-10592/
sources: SOURCE:	 Boundless.com. (n.d.). Social media. Retrieved November 13, 2015, from https://www.

questions.question: A marketer uses a headline in an advertisement that says, “Cures athlete’s foot in 2 hours”. The advertisement also includes a disclosure that says, “This product has not been shown to cure athlete’s foot in 2 hours.” Would the FTC approve of this headline and disclosure?

options.A: No, the disclosure and claim say the same thing.
options.B: Yes, the disclosure validates the claim.
options.C: Yes, as long as there is a disclosure, any claim is fine.
options.D: No, the disclosure cannot contradict the claim.

questions.explanation: No, the disclosure cannot contradict the claim. If the disclosure contradicts the claim, the claim must be modified because it is deceptive. This disclosure does not validate the claim, nor do they say the same thing. If a claim could be misleading to a reasonable consumer, the disclosure is used to remove any possibility of deception, but it cannot completely contradict the claim. http://www.ahbbo.com/adsftc.html
sources: SOURCE:	 AHBBO. (1998-2014). Not just 6 lines, 65 characters. Retrieved November 16, 2015, from

questions.question: While watching your favorite television show, you notice that the characters are shown drinking cans of Coca-Cola. This is an example of
questions.explanation: Product placement. Product placement is a type of “other” media that uses products as props in movies, television shows, and computer games. Telemarketing is an advertising medium grouped with “other” media that utilizes telephone service to promote a product either by phoning prospective customers or providing a number for them to call. Direct mail is a promotional medium that comes to consumers’ homes and businesses in the form of letters, catalogs, postcards, folders, faxes, and emails. Infomercials are lengthy commercials that look like television programs. 30
sources: SOURCE:	 PR LAP 3—Ad-Quipping Your Business (Types of Advertising)

questions.question: Word-of-mouth marketing is based on the idea that

options.A: consumers trust each other more than brands.
options.B: consumers trust brands over other consumers.
options.C: cheaper marketing is always more effective.

questions.explanation: Consumers trust each other more than brands. Word-of-mouth marketing is based on the idea that the power has shifted to the consumer because consumers trust each other more than they trust companies or brands, not that consumers trust brands over other consumers. Cheaper marketing is not always more effective, and paid advertising still works for the masses. Retrieved November 13, 2015, from http://compdb.mbaresearch.org/index.php/ indicator/1769
sources: SOURCE:	 University College Falmouth. (November 1, 2011). Introducing word of mouth marketing.

questions.question: Le’veon wants to create a marketing mix that includes both push and pull strategies. Which marketing mix should he choose?

options.A: Customer relationship management and trade shows
options.B: Mass media advertising and sales promotions
options.C: Getting retailers to stock the product and creating a supply chain

questions.explanation: Customer relationship management and trade shows. Customer relationship management is a pull strategy, while trade shows are a push strategy. Push strategies involve promoting products to intermediaries, who then promote the products to customers. Pull strategies involve promoting products directly to the ultimate consumer or industrial user; intermediaries then purchase the products from the manufacturer to meet customer demand. Mass media advertising and sales promotions are both pull strategies. Getting retailers to stock the product and creating a supply chain are both push strategies. Referrals and sales promotions are both pull strategies. boundless.com/marketing/textbooks/boundless-marketing-textbook/integrated-marketing- communications-12/selecting-the-promotion-mix-for-a-particular-product-84/push-and- pull-strategies-425-4128/\
sources: SOURCE:	 Boundless. (2015). Push and pull strategies. Retrieved November 14, 2015, from https://www.

questions.question: Sealway just launched a new caulk product that performs better than any other caulk product on the market. Which public relations activity should Sealway use to physically demonstrate the new performance features of its product?
questions.explanation: Trade shows. Participating in trade shows would allow Sealway to physically demonstrate the new performance features of its product to a targeted audience. Although blogs, newsletters, and press releases fall into the category of public relations, none of these activities would be able to physically demonstrate the new product features. ipr.org.uk/pr-tools-and-techniques.html
sources: SOURCE:	 IPR.org.uk. (2012). PR tools and techniques. Retrieved November 16, 2015, from http://www.

questions.question: You are creating a print advertisement for a new product and want to make it more readable and clear for your audience. What is one way to achieve this goal?

options.A: Use as many different fonts as possible
options.B: Fill up all white space on the page
options.C: Add multiple images of different products
options.D: Increase white space on the page

questions.explanation: Increase white space on the page. White space helps create focus, separates elements on the page, and promotes readability of an advertisement. Filling up white space, adding multiple images, and using as many different fonts as possible will negatively impact the readability and clarity of the advertisement. 2015, from http://www.serif.com/blog/how-important-is-white-space-in-print-design/
sources: SOURCE:	 Mauloni, M. (2015). How important is white space in print design? Retrieved November 16,

questions.question: A great way to demonstrate the viability of a good or service is to show that it worked in a real-world application. Which of the following public relations tactics would be the best way to do this?
questions.explanation: Case studies. Case studies allow a company to showcase a real-world application of its product or brand with proven results and statistics. White papers showcase a company’s expertise on a subject. Newsletters and press releases are great for awareness, but rarely contain the information to show the results of implementing a good or service for a client/company/consumer. mbaresearch.org/index.php/indicator/1773 31
sources: SOURCE:	 Meranus, R. (n.d.). Developing a PR plan. Retrieved November 16, 2015, from http://compdb.

questions.question: Harold is the Chief Operations Officer of a bottled water company. The FDA just issued a press release stating that the company’s bottled water product is contaminated. Which department should Harold turn to in order to handle this situation?
questions.explanation: Public relations. One of the responsibilities of the public relations department is to handle crisis communications. Human resources deals with planning, staffing, leading, and organizing employees of a business. Product development is concerned with the creation of a new product. Market research is the systematic gathering, recording, and analyzing of data about a specific issue, situation, or concern that affects a market. www.referenceforbusiness.com/small/Op-Qu/Public-Relations.html
sources: SOURCE:	 Reference for Business. (2015). Public relations. Retrieved November 17, 2015, from http://

questions.question: A sporting goods chain does such good business that it’s able to open another location in a new city. As a result, the chain is able to hire more salespeople, managers, and other people to work at the new location. What role of selling in our economy does this illustrate?
questions.explanation: Affects employment. When a business grows, it’s able to hire not just more salespeople but other employees, as well. The exchanges created by selling help businesses make a profit that enables the companies to expand. Selling aids utility, promotes competition, and helps customers determine needs, but those roles are not illustrated in this example.
sources: SOURCE:	 SE LAP 117—Sell Away (The Nature and Scope of Selling)

questions.question: When Cecelia’s company delivers a new piece of machinery to a manufacturing plant, she visits the plant to show employees how to use it. Cecelia is engaging in
questions.explanation: Post-sale customer service. A salesperson’s job isn’t over when the sale closes. The post-sale period is a critical time to flex your customer service muscle. This time of follow-through is what separates successful businesses from unsuccessful businesses. One way to take advantage of post-sale opportunities is by offering customer training. That’s what Cecelia is doing when she visits the plant and shows employees how to use their new machinery. Suggestion selling and providing ample product information are both considered pre-sale customer service, which is not what Cecelia’s engaging in.
sources: SOURCE:	 SE LAP 130—Go Beyond the Sale (Customer Service in Selling)

questions.question: Shivan has a great experience buying from a local florist, so he tells everyone he knows about the shop. The florist benefits from
questions.explanation: Word-of-mouth advertising. When someone has a great experience with a business, s/he often tells his/her friends, family, and coworkers. This is considered word-of-mouth advertising, or promotion and publicity for a business provided by customers who tell others of their satisfaction with the business. Word-of-mouth advertising is incredibly effective because customers tend to trust recommendations from people they know. Shivan’s word-of-mouth advertising can help the florist increase business. Reduced selling costs are another benefit that comes from having a reliable customer base. Personal satisfaction is a benefit to salespeople. Company leads are one way that businesses prospect for new customers.
sources: SOURCE:	 SE LAP 115—Keep Them Loyal (Building Clientele)

questions.question: Production capacity is what type of factor that can affect a business’s selling policies?
questions.explanation: Internal. Production capacity is an internal factor. It comes from inside the business itself, and the business has a measure of control over it. Regulatory factors are laws and guidelines put in place by government entities. External factors are factors in the business environment outside of a particular firm’s control. Production capacity is not necessarily a financial factor.

questions.question: Mark spreads an untrue rumor about his competition in an attempt to make his own company look better. Mark’s actions are considered
questions.explanation: Slander. Saying something untrue that damages a person’s or business’s reputation is considered slander. Negligence is not doing something necessary or required. Bribery is making illegal payments intended to secure business or products in return. Libel is writing something untrue that damages a person’s or business’s reputation. Negligence, bribery, and libel are illegal, but Mark isn’t engaging in them in this example. December 8, 2015, from http://inbusinessmag.com/in-business/slander-lawsuits- complicated-costly#.VmbxyHarSM8 32
sources: SOURCE:	 Kern-Fleischer, S. (2015, January). Slander lawsuits: Complicated and costly. Retrieved

questions.question: A salesperson may gain valuable product information by attending the Consumer Electronics Show. This is an example of a(n)
questions.explanation: Trade exhibition. The Consumer Electronics Show is an example of a trade exhibition, or a convention where salespeople and manufacturers come together to demonstrate products, exchange information, etc. A testimonial is a customer’s feedback about a product. An industry publication is a magazine or periodical. A blog is a personal website where someone can post thoughts, opinions, articles, etc.
sources: SOURCE:	 SE LAP 131—Get Informed (Acquiring Product Information for Use in Selling)

questions.question: A salesperson who tells a customer that an office chair is grey is pointing out a product
questions.explanation: Feature. A feature is a physical characteristic or quality of a product. It is something the customer can touch, feel, smell, see, or measure. It helps to describe the product (such as pointing out the color of an office chair). In short, a feature answers the question, “What is it?” A benefit is the personal satisfaction or advantage that a customer wants from a product. It is how a feature helps a particular buyer. The color of an office chair is not considered a product option or demonstration.
sources: SOURCE:	 SE LAP 113—Find Features, Boost Benefits (Feature-Benefit Selling)

questions.question: Many customers second-guess their purchasing decisions during which stage of purchasing?

options.D: Before the need for a product is identified 14 15 Marketing 2016 EXAM | ANSWER

questions.explanation: Post-purchase. It is common for customers to question their purchasing decisions after they purchase goods. Most consumers do not second-guess their decisions before the need for a product is identified or during the pre-purchase and during-purchase stages because they have not yet purchased the good.
